v.CNV: 众税吏和罪人，都接近耶稣，要听他讲道。
v.ESV: Now the tax collectors and sinners were all drawing near to hear him.
v.GAE: 모든 세리와 죄인들이 말씀을 들으러 가까이 나아오니
v.KJV: Then drew near unto him all the publicans and sinners for to hear him.
v.NASB: Now all the tax collectors and the sinners were coming near Him to listen to Him.
v.NIV: Now the tax collectors and sinners were all gathering around to hear Jesus.
v.NRSV: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming near to listen to him.
v.SAENEW: 세리들과 죄인들이 모두 예수의 말씀을 들으려고 그에게 가까이 몰려들었다.
v.WLB: 세리들과 죄인들이 모두 예수의 말씀을 듣기 위해 모여들었습니다.

v.CNV: 法利赛人和经学家，纷纷议论说：“这个人接待罪人，又和他们一起吃饭。”
v.ESV: And the Pharisees and the scribes grumbled, saying, “This man receives sinners and eats with them.”
v.GAE: 바리새인과 서기관들이 수군거려 이르되 이 사람이 죄인을 영접하고 음식을 같이 먹는다 하더라
v.KJV: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
v.NASB: Both the Pharisees and the scribes began to grumble, saying, 'This man receives sinners and eats with them.'
v.NIV: But the Pharisees and the teachers of the law muttered, “This man welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
v.NRSV: And the Pharisees and the scribes were grumbling and saying, “This fellow welcomes sinners and eats with them.”
v.SAENEW: 바리새파 사람들과 율법학자들은 투덜거리며 말하였다. "이 사람이 죄인들을 맞아들이고, 그들과 함께 음식을 먹는구나."
v.WLB: 그러자 바리새파 사람들과 율법학자들이 크게 웅성거리기 시작했습니다. “이 사람이 죄인들을 맞아들여 그들과 함께 음식을 먹는다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣就对他们讲了这个比喻，说：
v.ESV: So he told them this parable:
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들에게 이 비유로 이르시되
v.KJV: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
v.NASB: So He told them this parable, saying,
v.NIV: Then Jesus told them this parable:
v.NRSV: So he told them this parable:
v.SAENEW: 그래서 예수께서는 그들에게 이 비유를 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 그러자 예수께서 그들에게 이런 비유를 들려주셨습니다.

v.CNV: “你们中间谁有一百只羊，失去一只，不把九十九只留下在旷野，去寻找那失落的，直到找着呢？
v.ESV: “What man of you, having a hundred sheep, if he has lost one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open country, and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?
v.GAE: 너희 중에 어떤 사람이 양 백 마리가 있는데 그 중의 하나를 잃으면 아흔아홉 마리를 들에 두고 그 잃은 것을 찾아내기까지 찾아다니지 아니하겠느냐
v.KJV: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
v.NASB: 'What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and has lost one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go after the one which is lost until he finds it?
v.NIV: “Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them. Doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine in the open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it?
v.NRSV: “Which one of you, having a hundred sheep and losing one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the wilderness and go after the one that is lost until he finds it?
v.SAENEW: "너희 가운데서 어떤 사람이 양 백 마리를 가지고 있는데, 그 가운데서 한 마리를 잃으면, 아흔아홉 마리를 들에 두고, 그 잃은 양을 찾을 때까지 찾아 다니지 않겠느냐?
v.WLB: “너희 중 누가 100마리의 양을 가지고 있었는데 그중 한 마리를 잃어버렸다고 하자. 그러면 99마리의 양을 들판에 두고 그 잃어버린 양 한 마리를 찾을 때까지 찾아다니지 않겠느냐?

v.CNV: 既找着了，就欢欢喜喜地放在肩上，
v.ESV: And when he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
v.GAE: 또 찾아낸즉 즐거워 어깨에 메고
v.KJV: And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
v.NASB: 'When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
v.NIV: And when he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders
v.NRSV: When he has found it, he lays it on his shoulders and rejoices.
v.SAENEW: 찾으면, 기뻐하며 자기 어깨에 메고
v.WLB: 그리고 양을 찾게 되면 기뻐하며 양을 어깨에 메고

v.CNV: 回到家里，请朋友邻舍来，对他们说：‘请大家和我一同欢乐，因为我失去的羊已经找到了！’
v.ESV: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
v.GAE: 집에 와서 그 벗과 이웃을 불러 모으고 말하되 나와 함께 즐기자 나의 잃은 양을 찾아내었노라 하리라
v.KJV: And when he cometh home, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
v.NASB: 'And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost!'
v.NIV: and goes home. Then he calls his friends and neighbors together and says, ‘Rejoice with me; I have found my lost sheep.’
v.NRSV: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep that was lost.’
v.SAENEW: 집으로 돌아와서, 벗과 이웃 사람을 불러모으고, '나와 함께 기뻐해 주십시오. 잃었던 내 양을 찾았습니다' 하고 말할 것이다.
v.WLB: 집에 와서 친구들과 이웃을 불러 모아 ‘나와 함께 기뻐해 주십시오. 잃어버린 내 양을 찾았습니다’라고 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 我告诉你们，因为一个罪人悔改，天上也要这样为他欢乐，比为九十九个不用悔改的义人欢乐更大。
v.ESV: Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
v.GAE: 내가 너희에게 이르노니 이와 같이 죄인 한 사람이 회개하면 하늘에서는 회개할 것 없는 의인 아흔아홉으로 말미암아 기뻐하는 것보다 더하리라
v.KJV: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine just persons, which need no repentance.
v.NASB: 'I tell you that in the same way, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
v.NIV: I tell you that in the same way there will be more rejoicing in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who do not need to repent.
v.NRSV: Just so, I tell you, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.
v.SAENEW: 내가 너희에게 말한다. 이와 같이 하늘에서는, 회개할 필요가 없는 의인 아흔아홉보다, 회개하는 죄인 한 사람을 두고 더 기뻐할 것이다."
v.WLB: 내가 너희에게 말한다. 이와 같이 하늘에서는 회개할 필요 없는 의인 99명보다 회개하는 죄인 한 명을 두고 더 기뻐할 것이다.

v.CNV: “或是一个女人，有十个银币，失去一个，哪能不点上灯，打扫屋子，细细地找，直到找着呢？
v.ESV: “Or what woman, having ten silver coins, if she loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and seek diligently until she finds it?
v.GAE: 어떤 여자가 열 드라크마가 있는데 하나를 잃으면 등불을 켜고 집을 쓸며 찾아내기까지 부지런히 찾지 아니하겠느냐
v.KJV: Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?
v.NASB: 'Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
v.NIV: “Or suppose a woman has ten silver coins and loses one. Doesn’t she light a lamp, sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
v.NRSV: “Or what woman having ten silver coins, if she loses one of them, does not light a lamp, sweep the house, and search carefully until she finds it?
v.SAENEW: "어떤 여자에게 드라크마 열 닢이 있는데, 그가 그 가운데서 하나를 잃으면, 등불을 켜고, 온 집안을 쓸며, 그것을 찾을 때까지 샅샅이 뒤지지 않겠느냐?
v.WLB: 어떤 여자가 열 개의 드라크마 동전을 가지고 있다가 그중 하나를 잃어버렸다고 하자. 여인이 등불을 켜고 집 안을 쓸며 동전을 찾을 때까지 샅샅이 뒤지지 않겠느냐?

v.CNV: 既找着了，就请朋友邻舍来，说：‘请大家和我一同欢乐，因为我失去的那个银币，已经找到了。’
v.ESV: And when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
v.GAE: 또 찾아낸즉 벗과 이웃을 불러 모으고 말하되 나와 함께 즐기자 잃은 드라크마를 찾아내었노라 하리라
v.KJV: And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the piece which I had lost.
v.NASB: 'When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, 'Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin which I had lost!'
v.NIV: And when she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors together and says, ‘Rejoice with me; I have found my lost coin.’
v.NRSV: When she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, for I have found the coin that I had lost.’
v.SAENEW: 그래서 찾으면, 벗과 이웃 사람을 불러모으고 말하기를 '나와 함께 기뻐해 주십시오. 잃었던 드라크마를 찾았습니다' 할 것이다.
v.WLB: 그리고 동전을 찾게 되면 친구들과 이웃을 불러 모아 ‘나와 함께 기뻐해 주십시오. 내가 잃어버린 동전을 찾았습니다’라고 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 我告诉你们，因为一个罪人悔改，　神的使者也必这样为他欢乐。”
v.ESV: Just so, I tell you, there is joy before the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
v.GAE: 내가 너희에게 이르노니 이와 같이 죄인 한 사람이 회개하면 하나님의 사자들 앞에 기쁨이 되느니라
v.KJV: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.
v.NASB: 'In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.'
v.NIV: In the same way, I tell you, there is rejoicing in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
v.NRSV: Just so, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
v.SAENEW: 내가 너희에게 말한다. 이와 같이 회개하는 죄인 한 사람을 두고, 하나님의 천사들이 기뻐할 것이다."
v.WLB: 내가 너희에게 말한다. 이와 같이 회개하는 죄인 한 사람을 두고 하나님의 천사들이 기뻐할 것이다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣又说：“某人有两个儿子。
v.ESV: And he said, “There was a man who had two sons.
v.GAE: 또 이르시되 어떤 사람에게 두 아들이 있는데
v.KJV: And he said, A certain man had two sons:
v.NASB: And He said, 'A man had two sons.
v.NIV: Jesus continued: “There was a man who had two sons.
v.NRSV: Then Jesus said, “There was a man who had two sons.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 말씀하셨다. "어떤 사람에게 아들이 둘 있는데
v.WLB: 예수께서 말씀하셨습니다. “어떤 사람에게 두 아들이 있었다.

v.CNV: 小儿子对父亲说：‘爸爸，请把我应得的家业给我。’父亲就把财产分给他们两兄弟。
v.ESV: And the younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of property that is coming to me.’ And he divided his property between them.
v.GAE: 그 둘째가 아버지에게 말하되 아버지여 재산 중에서 내게 돌아올 분깃을 내게 주소서 하는지라 아버지가 그 살림을 각각 나눠 주었더니
v.KJV: And the younger of them said to his father, Father, give me the portion of goods that falleth to me. And he divided unto them his living.
v.NASB: 'The younger of them said to his father, 'Father, give me the share of the estate that falls to me.' So he divided his wealth between them.
v.NIV: The younger one said to his father, ‘Father, give me my share of the estate.’ So he divided his property between them.
v.NRSV: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the property that will belong to me.’ So he divided his property between them.
v.SAENEW: 작은 아들이 아버지에게 말하기를 '아버지, 재산 가운데서 내게 돌아올 몫을 내게 주십시오' 하였다. 그래서 아버지는 살림을 두 아들에게 나누어 주었다.
v.WLB: 작은아들이 아버지에게 말했다. ‘아버지, 재산 중에서 제가 받을 몫을 주십시오.’ 그래서 아버지는 두 아들에게 살림을 나눠 주었다.

v.CNV: 过了不多几天，小儿子收拾一切，到远方去了，在那里生活放荡，浪费钱财。
v.ESV: Not many days later, the younger son gathered all he had and took a journey into a far country, and there he squandered his property in reckless living.
v.GAE: 그 후 며칠이 안 되어 둘째 아들이 재물을 다 모아 가지고 먼 나라에 가 거기서 허랑방탕하여 그 재산을 낭비하더니
v.KJV: And not many days after the younger son gathered all together, and took his journey into a far country, and there wasted his substance with riotous living.
v.NASB: 'And not many days later, the younger son gathered everything together and went on a journey into a distant country, and there he squandered his estate with loose living.
v.NIV: “Not long after that, the younger son got together all he had, set off for a distant country and there squandered his wealth in wild living.
v.NRSV: A few days later the younger son gathered all he had and traveled to a distant country, and there he squandered his property in dissolute living.
v.SAENEW: 며칠 뒤에 작은 아들은 제 것을 다 챙겨서 먼 지방으로 가서, 거기서 방탕하게 살면서, 그 재산을 낭비하였다.
v.WLB: 며칠 뒤 작은아들은 자기가 가진 것을 모두 챙겨서 멀리 다른 나라로 떠났다. 그러고는 거기서 방탕하게 살면서 그 재산을 낭비했다.

v.CNV: 他花尽了一切所有的，那地方又遇上了严重的饥荒，就穷困起来；
v.ESV: And when he had spent everything, a severe famine arose in that country, and he began to be in need.
v.GAE: 다 없앤 후 그 나라에 크게 흉년이 들어 그가 비로소 궁핍한지라
v.KJV: And when he had spent all, there arose a mighty famine in that land; and he began to be in want.
v.NASB: 'Now when he had spent everything, a severe famine occurred in that country, and he began to be impoverished.
v.NIV: After he had spent everything, there was a severe famine in that whole country, and he began to be in need.
v.NRSV: When he had spent everything, a severe famine took place throughout that country, and he began to be in need.
v.SAENEW: 그가 모든 것을 탕진했을 때에, 그 지방에 크게 흉년이 들어서, 그는 아주 궁핍하게 되었다.
v.WLB: 그가 모든 것을 탕진했을 때 그 나라 전역에 심한 흉년이 들어 형편이 어려워지기 시작했다.

v.CNV: 于是他去投靠当地的一个居民。那人打发他到田里去放猪，
v.ESV: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
v.GAE: 가서 그 나라 백성 중 한 사람에게 붙여 사니 그가 그를 들로 보내어 돼지를 치게 하였는데
v.KJV: And he went and joined himself to a citizen of that country; and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
v.NASB: 'So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed swine.
v.NIV: So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
v.NRSV: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, who sent him to his fields to feed the pigs.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그는 그 지방의 주민 가운데 한 사람을 찾아가서, 몸을 의탁하였다. 그 사람은 그를 들로 보내서 돼지를 치게 하였다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그는 그 나라 사람에게 일자리를 얻었는데 그 사람은 그를 들판으로 내보내 돼지를 치게 했다.

v.CNV: 他恨不得吃猪所吃的豆荚，可是没有人给他。
v.ESV: And he was longing to be fed with the pods that the pigs ate, and no one gave him anything.
v.GAE: 그가 돼지 먹는 쥐엄 열매로 배를 채우고자 하되 주는 자가 없는지라
v.KJV: And he would fain have filled his belly with the husks that the swine did eat: and no man gave unto him.
v.NASB: 'And he would have gladly filled his stomach with the pods that the swine were eating, and no one was giving anything to him.
v.NIV: He longed to fill his stomach with the pods that the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything.
v.NRSV: He would gladly have filled himself with the pods that the pigs were eating; and no one gave him anything.
v.SAENEW: 그는 돼지가 먹는 쥐엄 열매라도 좀 먹고 배를 채우고 싶은 심정이었으나, 그에게 먹을 것을 주는 사람이 없었다.
v.WLB: 그는 돼지가 먹는 쥐엄나무 열매로라도 배를 채우고 싶었지만 그것마저 주는 사람이 없었다.

v.CNV: 他醒悟过来，说：‘我父亲有那么多雇工，又有丰富的食物，我却要在这里饿死吗？
v.ESV: “But when he came to himself, he said, ‘How many of my father's hired servants have more than enough bread, but I perish here with hunger!
v.GAE: 이에 스스로 돌이켜 이르되 내 아버지에게는 양식이 풍족한 품꾼이 얼마나 많은가 나는 여기서 주려 죽는구나
v.KJV: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired servants of my father’s have bread enough and to spare, and I perish with hunger!
v.NASB: 'But when he came to his senses, he said, 'How many of my father's hired men have more than enough bread, but I am dying here with hunger!
v.NIV: “When he came to his senses, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired servants have food to spare, and here I am starving to death!
v.NRSV: But when he came to himself he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired hands have bread enough and to spare, but here I am dying of hunger!
v.SAENEW: 그제서야 그는 제정신이 들어서, 이렇게 말하였다. '내 아버지의 그 많은 품꾼들에게는 먹을 것이 남아도는데, 나는 여기서 굶어 죽는구나.
v.WLB: 그제야 제정신이 들어서 말했다. ‘내 아버지 집에는 양식이 풍부해서 일꾼들이 먹고도 남는데 나는 여기서 굶어 죽는구나!

v.CNV: 我要起来，到我父亲那里去，对他说：爸爸，我得罪了天，也得罪了你，
v.ESV: I will arise and go to my father, and I will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you.
v.GAE: 내가 일어나 아버지께 가서 이르기를 아버지 내가 하늘과 아버지께 죄를 지었사오니
v.KJV: I will arise and go to my father, and will say unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and before thee,
v.NASB: 'I will get up and go to my father, and will say to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight;
v.NIV: I will set out and go back to my father and say to him: Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you.
v.NRSV: I will get up and go to my father, and I will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you;
v.SAENEW: 내가 일어나 아버지에게 돌아가서, 이렇게 말씀드려야 하겠다. 아버지, 내가 하늘과 아버지 앞에 죄를 지었습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 일어나 아버지에게 돌아가 말해야겠다. 아버지, 제가 하늘과 아버지께 죄를 지었습니다.

v.CNV: 不配再称为你的儿子，把我当作一个雇工吧！’
v.ESV: I am no longer worthy to be called your son. Treat me as one of your hired servants.”’
v.GAE: 지금부터는 아버지의 아들이라 일컬음을 감당하지 못하겠나이다 나를 품꾼의 하나로 보소서 하리라 하고
v.KJV: And am no more worthy to be called thy son: make me as one of thy hired servants.
v.NASB: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me as one of your hired men.''
v.NIV: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; make me like one of your hired servants.’
v.NRSV: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired hands.”’
v.SAENEW: 나는 더 이상 아버지의 아들이라고 불릴 자격이 없으니, 나를 품꾼의 하나로 삼아 주십시오.'
v.WLB: 저는 더 이상 아버지의 아들이라 불릴 자격이 없으니 그저 하나의 일꾼으로나 삼아 주십시오.’

v.CNV: 于是他起来往父亲那里去。他还在远处时，他父亲看见了他，就动了慈心，跑过去抱着他，连连与他亲嘴。
v.ESV: And he arose and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
v.GAE: 이에 일어나서 아버지께로 돌아가니라 아직도 거리가 먼데 아버지가 그를 보고 측은히 여겨 달려가 목을 안고 입을 맞추니
v.KJV: And he arose, and came to his father. But when he was yet a great way off, his father saw him, and had compassion, and ran, and fell on his neck, and kissed him.
v.NASB: 'So he got up and came to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion for him, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
v.NIV: So he got up and went to his father. “But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion for him; he ran to his son, threw his arms around him and kissed him.
v.NRSV: So he set off and went to his father. But while he was still far off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion; he ran and put his arms around him and kissed him.
v.SAENEW: 그는 일어나서, 아버지에게로 갔다. 그가 아직도 먼 거리에 있는데, 그의 아버지가 그를 보고 측은히 여겨서, 달려가 그의 목을 껴안고, 입을 맞추었다.
v.WLB: 그러고서 아들은 일어나 아버지에게로 갔다. 아들이 아직 멀리 있는데 그 아버지는 아들을 보고 불쌍히 여겨 아들에게 달려가 그의 목을 껴안고 입을 맞췄다.

v.CNV: 儿子说：‘爸爸，我得罪了天，也得罪了你，不配再称为你的儿子。’
v.ESV: And the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
v.GAE: 아들이 이르되 아버지 내가 하늘과 아버지께 죄를 지었사오니 지금부터는 아버지의 아들이라 일컬음을 감당하지 못하겠나이다 하나
v.KJV: And the son said unto him, Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in thy sight, and am no more worthy to be called thy son.
v.NASB: 'And the son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.'
v.NIV: “The son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
v.NRSV: Then the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
v.SAENEW: 아들이 아버지에게 말하였다. '아버지, 내가 하늘과 아버지 앞에 죄를 지었습니다. 이제부터 나는 아버지의 아들이라고 불릴 자격이 없습니다.'
v.WLB: 아들이 아버지에게 말했다. ‘아버지, 제가 하늘과 아버지께 죄를 지었습니다. 이제 아들이라고도 불릴 자격이 없습니다.’

v.CNV: 父亲却吩咐仆人说：‘快把那最好的袍子拿来给他穿，把戒指戴在他手上，把鞋穿在他脚上，
v.ESV: But the father said to his servants, ‘Bring quickly the best robe, and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet.
v.GAE: 아버지는 종들에게 이르되 제일 좋은 옷을 내어다가 입히고 손에 가락지를 끼우고 발에 신을 신기라
v.KJV: But the father said to his servants, Bring forth the best robe, and put it on him; and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet:
v.NASB: 'But the father said to his slaves, 'Quickly bring out the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand and sandals on his feet;
v.NIV: “But the father said to his servants, ‘Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
v.NRSV: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Quickly, bring out a robe — the best one — and put it on him; put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 아버지는 종들에게 말하였다. '어서, 가장 좋은 옷을 꺼내서, 그에게 입히고, 손에 반지를 끼우고, 발에 신을 신겨라.
v.WLB: 그러나 아버지는 종들에게 말했다. ‘어서 가장 좋은 옷을 가져와 이 아이에게 입혀라. 손가락에 반지를 끼우고 발에 신을 신겨라.

v.CNV: 把肥牛犊牵来宰了，我们要吃喝快乐，
v.ESV: And bring the fattened calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate.
v.GAE: 그리고 살진 송아지를 끌어다가 잡으라 우리가 먹고 즐기자
v.KJV: And bring hither the fatted calf, and kill it; and let us eat, and be merry:
v.NASB: and bring the fattened calf, kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
v.NIV: Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let’s have a feast and celebrate.
v.NRSV: And get the fatted calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate;
v.SAENEW: 그리고 살진 송아지를 끌어내다가 잡아라. 우리가 먹고 즐기자.
v.WLB: 살진 송아지를 끌어다 잡아라. 잔치를 벌이고 즐기자.

v.CNV: 因为我这儿子是死而复活、失而又得的。’他们就欢乐起来。
v.ESV: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found.’ And they began to celebrate.
v.GAE: 이 내 아들은 죽었다가 다시 살아났으며 내가 잃었다가 다시 얻었노라 하니 그들이 즐거워하더라
v.KJV: For this my son was dead, and is alive again; he was lost, and is found. And they began to be merry.
v.NASB: for this son of mine was dead and has come to life again; he was lost and has been found.' And they began to celebrate.
v.NIV: For this son of mine was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’ So they began to celebrate.
v.NRSV: for this son of mine was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found!’ And they began to celebrate.
v.SAENEW: 나의 이 아들은 죽었다가 살아났고, 내가 잃었다가 되찾았다.' 그래서 그들은 잔치를 벌였다.
v.WLB: 내 아들이 죽었다가 다시 살아났다. 이 아들을 잃었다가 이제 찾았다.’ 이렇게 그들은 잔치를 벌이기 시작했다.

v.CNV: “那时，大儿子正在田里。他回来离家不远的时候，听见音乐跳舞的声音，
v.ESV: “Now his older son was in the field, and as he came and drew near to the house, he heard music and dancing.
v.GAE: 맏아들은 밭에 있다가 돌아와 집에 가까이 왔을 때에 풍악과 춤추는 소리를 듣고
v.KJV: Now his elder son was in the field: and as he came and drew nigh to the house, he heard musick and dancing.
v.NASB: 'Now his older son was in the field, and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
v.NIV: “Meanwhile, the older son was in the field. When he came near the house, he heard music and dancing.
v.NRSV: “Now his elder son was in the field; and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
v.SAENEW: 그런데 큰 아들이 밭에 있다가 돌아오는데, 집에 가까이 이르렀을 때에, 음악 소리와 춤추면서 노는 소리를 듣고,
v.WLB: 그런데 큰아들은 들에 나가 있었다. 그가 집 가까이에 이르렀을 때 음악과 춤추는 소리가 들렸다.

v.CNV: 就叫了一个仆人来，问他这是怎么一回事。
v.ESV: And he called one of the servants and asked what these things meant.
v.GAE: 한 종을 불러 이 무슨 일인가 물은대
v.KJV: And he called one of the servants, and asked what these things meant.
v.NASB: 'And he summoned one of the servants and began inquiring what these things could be.
v.NIV: So he called one of the servants and asked him what was going on.
v.NRSV: He called one of the slaves and asked what was going on.
v.SAENEW: 종 하나를 불러서, 무슨 일인지를 물어 보았다.
v.WLB: 그래서 하인 하나를 불러 무슨 일인지 물어 보았다.

v.CNV: 仆人说：‘你弟弟回来了，你父亲因为他平安无恙地回来，就宰了肥牛犊。’
v.ESV: And he said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he has received him back safe and sound.’
v.GAE: 대답하되 당신의 동생이 돌아왔으매 당신의 아버지가 건강한 그를 다시 맞아들이게 됨으로 인하여 살진 송아지를 잡았나이다 하니
v.KJV: And he said unto him, Thy brother is come; and thy father hath killed the fatted calf, because he hath received him safe and sound.
v.NASB: 'And he said to him, 'Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has received him back safe and sound.'
v.NIV: ‘Your brother has come,’ he replied, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf because he has him back safe and sound.’
v.NRSV: He replied, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fatted calf, because he has got him back safe and sound.’
v.SAENEW: 종이 그에게 말하였다. '아우님이 집에 돌아왔습니다. 건강한 몸으로 돌아온 것을 반겨서, 주인 어른께서 살진 송아지를 잡으셨습니다.'
v.WLB: 하인이 대답했다. ‘동생이 왔습니다. 동생이 건강하게 무사히 돌아와서 주인 어른께서 살진 송아지를 잡으셨습니다.’

v.CNV: 大儿子就生气，不肯进去；父亲出来劝他。
v.ESV: But he was angry and refused to go in. His father came out and entreated him,
v.GAE: 그가 노하여 들어가고자 하지 아니하거늘 아버지가 나와서 권한대
v.KJV: And he was angry, and would not go in: therefore came his father out, and intreated him.
v.NASB: 'But he became angry and was not willing to go in; and his father came out and began pleading with him.
v.NIV: “The older brother became angry and refused to go in. So his father went out and pleaded with him.
v.NRSV: Then he became angry and refused to go in. His father came out and began to plead with him.
v.SAENEW: 큰 아들은 화가 나서, 집으로 들어가려고 하지 않았다. 아버지가 나와서 그를 달랬다.
v.WLB: 큰아들은 화가 나서 들어가려 하지 않았다. 그러자 아버지가 나와 그를 달랬다.

v.CNV: 他对父亲说：‘你看，我服事你这么多年，从来没有违背过你的命令，可是你没有给我一只山羊羔，叫我和朋友一同欢乐。
v.ESV: but he answered his father, ‘Look, these many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed your command, yet you never gave me a young goat, that I might celebrate with my friends.
v.GAE: 아버지께 대답하여 이르되 내가 여러 해 아버지를 섬겨 명을 어김이 없거늘 내게는 염소 새끼라도 주어 나와 내 벗으로 즐기게 하신 일이 없더니
v.KJV: And he answering said to his father, Lo, these many years do I serve thee, neither transgressed I at any time thy commandment: and yet thou never gavest me a kid, that I might make merry with my friends:
v.NASB: 'But he answered and said to his father, 'Look! For so many years I have been serving you and I have never neglected a command of yours; and yet you have never given me a young goat, so that I might celebrate with my friends;
v.NIV: But he answered his father, ‘Look! All these years I’ve been slaving for you and never disobeyed your orders. Yet you never gave me even a young goat so I could celebrate with my friends.
v.NRSV: But he answered his father, ‘Listen! For all these years I have been working like a slave for you, and I have never disobeyed your command; yet you have never given me even a young goat so that I might celebrate with my friends.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 그는 아버지에게 대답하였다. '나는 이렇게 여러 해를 두고 아버지를 섬기고 있고, 아버지의 명령을 한 번도 어긴 일이 없는데, 나에게는 친구들과 함께 즐기라고, 염소 새끼 한 마리도 주신 일이 없습니다.
v.WLB: 그러자 큰아들이 아버지에게 이렇게 말했다. ‘보십시오! 저는 여러 해 동안 아버지를 위해 종 노릇 하고 무슨 말씀이든 어긴 적이 없습니다. 그런데 제게는 친구들과 함께 즐기라며 염소 새끼 한 마리도 주시지 않았습니다.

v.CNV: 但你这个儿子，常常跟娼妓在一起，花尽了你的财产，他一回来，你倒为他宰杀肥牛犊！’
v.ESV: But when this son of yours came, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him!’
v.GAE: 아버지의 살림을 창녀들과 함께 삼켜 버린 이 아들이 돌아오매 이를 위하여 살진 송아지를 잡으셨나이다
v.KJV: But as soon as this thy son was come, which hath devoured thy living with harlots, thou hast killed for him the fatted calf.
v.NASB: but when this son of yours came, who has devoured your wealth with prostitutes, you killed the fattened calf for him.'
v.NIV: But when this son of yours who has squandered your property with prostitutes comes home, you kill the fattened calf for him!’
v.NRSV: But when this son of yours came back, who has devoured your property with prostitutes, you killed the fatted calf for him!’
v.SAENEW: 그런데 창녀들과 어울려서 아버지의 재산을 다 삼켜 버린 이 아들이 오니까, 그를 위해서는 살진 송아지를 잡으셨습니다.'
v.WLB: 그런데 창녀와 함께 아버지의 재산을 탕진한 아들이 집에 돌아오니까 아버지는 그를 위해 살진 송아지를 잡으셨습니다.’

v.CNV: 父亲对他说：‘孩子，你常跟我在一起，我的一切都是你的。
v.ESV: And he said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
v.GAE: 아버지가 이르되 얘 너는 항상 나와 함께 있으니 내 것이 다 네 것이로되
v.KJV: And he said unto him, Son, thou art ever with me, and all that I have is thine.
v.NASB: 'And he said to him, 'Son, you have always been with me, and all that is mine is yours.
v.NIV: “‘My son,’ the father said, ‘you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
v.NRSV: Then the father said to him, ‘Son, you are always with me, and all that is mine is yours.
v.SAENEW: 아버지가 그에게 말하였다. '얘야, 너는 늘 나와 함께 있으니 내가 가진 모든 것은 다 네 것이다.
v.WLB: 아버지가 말했다. ‘얘야, 너는 항상 나와 함께 있지 않느냐? 또 내가 가진 모든 것이 다 네 것이다.

v.CNV: 只是因为你这个弟弟是死而复活、失而又得的，我们应该欢喜快乐。’”
v.ESV: It was fitting to celebrate and be glad, for this your brother was dead, and is alive; he was lost, and is found.’”
v.GAE: 이 네 동생은 죽었다가 살아났으며 내가 잃었다가 얻었기로 우리가 즐거워하고 기뻐하는 것이 마땅하다 하니라
v.KJV: It was meet that we should make merry, and be glad: for this thy brother was dead, and is alive again; and was lost, and is found.
v.NASB: 'But we had to celebrate and rejoice, for this brother of yours was dead and has begun to live, and was lost and has been found.''
v.NIV: But we had to celebrate and be glad, because this brother of yours was dead and is alive again; he was lost and is found.’”
v.NRSV: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and has come to life; he was lost and has been found.’”
v.SAENEW: 그런데 너의 이 아우는 죽었다가 살아났고, 내가 잃었다가 되찾았으니, 즐기며 기뻐하는 것이 마땅하다.'"
v.WLB: 그러나 네 동생은 죽었다가 다시 살아났고 내가 그를 잃었다가 찾았으니 우리가 잔치를 벌이며 기뻐하는 것이 당연하다.’”
